notes_md:
**mi** = eyelash (the tiny hairs on your eyelids).  

It’s a noun. You often hear **lông mi** to be extra clear, and you can count it as **một sợi mi** (one lash) or talk about **hàng mi** (a row of lashes).  

Common pairings: **mi dài**, **mi cong**, **chuốt mi**, **kẹp mi**.  

Be careful: **mi** (eyelash) is different from **mì** (noodles). The tone changes the meaning completely.
exampleSentencesTranslated: You can use mascara to make your eyelash longer.
I got dust in my eyelash so it feels a bit uncomfortable.
I like looking at your naturally curved eyelash every morning.
exampleSentences: Chị có thể dùng mascara để làm cho mi dài hơn.
Em bị bụi bay vào mi nên cảm thấy hơi khó chịu.
Anh thích ngắm mi cong tự nhiên của em mỗi sáng.
